text: Image and video fusion aims to combine the complementary information from multiple sources into a single representation by exploiting their redundancies and complementary aspects, respectively. However, the extraction of complementary information from different sources, such as infrared and visible images, is a challenging task due to the large modality gap between them. Most of the existing cross-attention-based methods rely heavily on the correlation between different modalities, and even focusing on complementary information in image fusion is often overlooked, which leads to unsatisfactory performance. To explore the complementary information from different modalities, a new Cross-Attention Mechanism (CAM) is designed. We adopt a two-stage training approach: 1) training independent auto-encoder networks for different modalities, 2) training the CAM and a shared decoder network with the pre-trained encoders. By using CAM, the complementary features of different modalities can be fully fused into one representation for further improvement. Our experimental results show that the proposed method achieves the new state-of-the-art performance compared with the existing works on several benchmark datasets. The code will be open to the public.
